evals.name: fresh-scaffold-dotnet
evals.prompt: I just spun up a new ASP.NET Core Web API called OrderService (net8.0, has a .sln + one csproj under src/OrderService, plain git init, no CLAUDE.md yet). Before the team starts building on it I want the whole AI-dev constitution set up — rules, OKF docs, backlog, the works.
evals.expected_output: Fresh-scaffold mode: dotnet profile confirmed, all owned rules copied verbatim, manifest at current VERSION, every Step 4 artifact scaffolded with no unresolved placeholders (adr template carve-out excepted), nothing committed. The .claude/rules/ directory is scaffolded (empty). The glossary is seeded with at least one term row derived from the repo's own domain (OrderService), confirmed rather than left as an empty table.
evals.assertions: see grade.py grade_fresh — derived from skill source at grade time

evals.name: legacy-migration
evals.prompt: This repo (InvoiceApi) already has a CLAUDE.md we wrote by hand a year ago — it's got some architecture constraints and our bl/NNN-short-description branch naming convention in it, but nothing else, no ADRs, no journal, no backlog. Bring it up to our standard AI-dev setup without losing what's already written in there.
evals.expected_output: Legacy-migration mode: CLAUDE.md rewritten around the @import block with all original repo-specific content preserved verbatim somewhere in the result (architecture constraints, branch convention), remaining artifacts scaffolded, manifest written, nothing committed. The Step 7 report (saved by the eval harness to <ws>/legacy-migration/outputs/step7-report.md, outside the repo) carries a "### Constitution candidates" section quoting the decimal-money constraint with its source path, and does NOT propose the bl/NNN branch convention (project-instance data, not generalizable law). Law-shaped constraints (the decimal-money rule) are carved into .claude/rules/<topic>.md; instance data (the bl/NNN branch convention) stays in CLAUDE.md. The glossary is seeded with at least one term row derived from the InvoiceApi domain (invoices, webhooks); definition-like legacy content becomes glossary rows.

evals.name: upgrade
evals.prompt: We changed the Legislator's core rules and bumped VERSION since this repo was last legislated. Re-run it here so the repo picks up the update. Also mark docs/notes/perf-tuning.md as protected: add it to the manifest keep list with reason exactly "hand-tuned GC settings notes".
evals.expected_output: Upgrade mode: profiles reused from manifest without re-asking, the rules added since last legislation are copied in (one core, one dotnet stack), the retired rule is deleted (deletion propagation), manifest bumped to current VERSION with exact sorted ownedFiles, project-owned files (including CLAUDE.md) untouched, nothing committed. Fixture is generated by setup_workspace.py from the current skill source: it withholds the alphabetically last core rule AND the alphabetically last dotnet stack rule, and plants a retired-rule.md. The Step 7 report (saved by the eval harness to <ws>/upgrade/outputs/step7-report.md, outside the repo) proposes the @import line for the newly delivered stack rule under Needs your review. The manifest's existing keep entry (deploy-runbook) survives byte-identically and the new perf-tuning entry is added in sorted position, pinned single-line serialization.
evals.assertions: see grade.py grade_upgrade — uses fixture_meta.json written at setup time

evals.name: idempotency
evals.prompt: (second run, same repo, after committing run 1's result) Re-run the legislator here — nothing about the constitution or the repo has changed.
evals.expected_output: Zero diff: git status and git diff HEAD both empty after the second run. This is the constitution's core promise and the cheapest regression signal for serialization/formatting drift. Run on both the fresh-scaffold repo and the upgrade repo — the upgrade repo's populated keep list must re-serialize byte-identically. Also run on the restructure repo after run 1 is committed — an already-standard layer must yield a no-item plan (open decision items excepted) and zero writes.

evals.name: audit
evals.prompt: Something feels off with the AI docs in this repo — audit the AI layer here and tell me what's rotted. Don't change anything, just report.
evals.expected_output: Full read-only audit: a severity-ranked report naming all fourteen planted defects (broken ghost-rule import, unresolved {{PROJECT_OVERVIEW}} token, drifted okf.md owned file, manifest one version behind, stale renamed-away.md index link, stale legacy/ map row, orphan-notes.md orphan, journal dead since 2026-01-15 while code moved on, .cursorrules foreign structure, keep-listed special-sauce.md referenced nowhere, a .claude/rules/journal.md project rule contradicting the dev-journal law, the stray docs/superpowers/review-checklist.md rulebook flagged under the stray-rulebooks slug, the empty glossary flagged under the glossary-vitality slug, the root UBIQUITOUS_LANGUAGE.md foreign glossary store flagged under foreign-structures — severity is the agent's call on a mixed law/definition file). Zero writes: git status and HEAD identical before and after. In evals the agent saves the report to <ws>/rotted-layer/outputs/audit-report.md — outside the target repo. The report must not flag docs/okf/index.md or docs/okf/glossary.md as orphans. The report carries a Constitution candidates appendix before the Clean checks line quoting the dry-run data-imports rule AND the stray rulebook's reversible-migrations rule (the foreign glossary's past-tense rule is check-9 territory, not a harvest source, and is expected in the finding text instead); the not-law-marked statement about deleting invoice rows is suppressed and appears nowhere; the candidates section proposes neither the stray rulebook's project-specific PdfRenderer line, nor the foreign glossary's billing-period instance definition, nor the contradicting journal.md line (a contradiction is covered by the rule it contradicts).
evals.assertions: see grade.py grade_audit — markers from fixture_meta.json

evals.name: restructure
evals.prompt: The AI docs in this repo have rotted and there's leftover config from other AI tools in the way. Restructure the AI layer into the standard shape. I pre-approve applying everything you propose except items that need my decision — leave those open and show them in the report.
evals.expected_output: Diagnose (full audit, zero writes) -> plan in the pinned format -> apply approved items -> fidelity pass. Zero content loss (every planted sentence still greppable), the kept file byte-identical in place, the CHANGELOG conflict surfaced as an open [decision] item and NOT auto-resolved, .claude/plans relocated to docs/superpowers/plans, .cursorrules merged into CLAUDE.md and removed, the stray docs/superpowers/review-checklist.md rulebook merged into .claude/rules/<topic>.md (its PdfRenderer law greppable there) and the file removed, the foreign UBIQUITOUS_LANGUAGE.md store merged away (its billing-period definition becomes a docs/okf/glossary.md row; its generic law line survives per fidelity), ghost-rule import fixed, the empty glossary seeded via the Step 4 derivation rule as an approval-gated fix item (>=1 term row afterwards), owned drift healed via Steps 2-3 (manifest at current VERSION, keep list carried). The conflicting .claude/rules/journal.md is a second open [decision] item — its content byte-unchanged. The report ends with 'Fidelity: verified (<N> lines tracked)' and is saved by the eval harness to <ws>/restructure/outputs/restructure-report.md.
evals.assertions: see grade.py grade_restructure — fidelity_sentences/conflict_marker/kept_* from fixture_meta.json
